findings.title: Open redirect reachable through the enhanced shell sign-in flow
findings.description: Same open-redirect class as in app.ts, via the shell's interactive path. The /login route reads `context.search.next` straight from the query string (L75) and seeds it into the login form, and createReferenceAppShell wires the sign-in mutation response with `redirectTo: (result) => authRedirectTo(result.value)` (L86, L106). authRedirectTo (L180-187) returns `result.value.redirectTo` verbatim, which is the better-auth `redirectPath(input.next, ...)` output. Because redirectPath (packages/better-auth/src/index.ts:1204-1209) blocks `//` but not a leading `/\`, a link such as `/login?next=/\evil.com` causes the shell's request handler to emit `Location: /\evil.com`, which browsers normalize to `https://evil.com` after the victim authenticates. The scanner's xss flags (L62 controlled client-module href; L76 next is escapeAttribute-escaped in the form; L112/L132 routeValueToHtml wrapping) and insecure-crypto flags (L53/L71 are meta description/title strings) are all false positives.
findings.recommendation: Fix redirectPath upstream to reject backslash-prefixed and control-character values (see app.ts recommendation), and/or validate `context.search.next` against a same-origin path allowlist in the /login route before passing it into the form and redirect.
